channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Worst Morning of My Developer Life — A Patient Hacker, a Fake AI Tool, and 150 Deleted Repos — My Story
item.description: I Woke Up to 150+ Deleted GitHub Repos and a Ransom Note — This Is What Happened A real developer. A real attack. A real recovery. I almost didn't write this. Partly because it's embarrassing....

item.title: Obsidotion — Bridge Obsidian and Notion Without Losing Your Notes
item.description: Are you stuck between Obsidian and Notion? Love Obsidian's local-first markdown but miss Notion's databases? What if you could use both? Obsidotion bridges the gap. It converts and syncs between...

item.title: OpenClaw 2026.3.31: Task Flows, Locked-Down Installs, and the Security Release Your Agent Needed
item.description: OpenClaw 2026.3.31 dropped yesterday, and this one's different. Where the last few releases added capabilities — new channels, new models, new tools — this release is about control. Specifically:...

item.title: Tired of Zillow Blocking Scrapers — Here's What Actually Works in 2026
item.description: If you've ever tried scraping Zillow with BeautifulSoup or Selenium, you know the pain. CAPTCHAs, IP bans, constantly changing HTML selectors, headless browser detection — it's an arms race you're...

item.title: Why Standard HTTP Libraries Are Dead for Web Scraping (And How to Fix It)
item.description: If you are building a data extraction pipeline in 2026 and your core network request looks like Ruby’s Net::HTTP.get(URI(url)) or Python's requests.get(url), you are already blocked. The era of...

item.title: 🦀 Rust Foundations — The Stuff That Finally Made Things Click
item.description: "Rust compiler and Clippy are the biggest tsunderes — they'll shout at you for every small mistake, but in the end… they just want your code to be perfect." Why I Even Started Rust I didn't pick...

item.title: Pomota — A Distraction-Free Pomodoro Timer You Can Self-Host
item.description: The Pomodoro Technique works. But most Pomodoro apps do not. They are either subscription-based, bloated with features, or require accounts. You just want a timer. Pomota gives you exactly that....

item.title: Day 61 of #100DaysOfCode — Python Refresher Part 1
item.description: When I started my #100DaysOfCode journey, I began with frontend development using React, then moved into backend development with Node.js and Express. After that, I explored databases to...

item.title: Sharing Two Open-Source Projects for Local AI & Secure LLM Access 🚀
item.description: Hey everyone! I’m finally jumping into the dev.to community. To kick things off, I wanted to share two tools I’ve been developing at the University of Jaén that tackle two common headaches in the...

item.title: Claude Code: how to use custom slash commands for your project
item.description: Claude Code: how to use custom slash commands for your project Claude Code ships with built-in slash commands like /compact, /clear, and /review. But the real power is in custom project commands —...

item.title: Anker’s Nebula P1 projector is the portable sound king
item.description: The Soundcore Nebula P1 from Anker isn't the most portable Google TV projector I've ever reviewed, nor is it the brightest. It doesn't even have a built-in battery. Instead it's a decent video...

item.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
item.description: Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and...

item.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
item.description: Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude...

item.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jurassic Fish Chokes on Squid
item.description: Here’s a fossil of a 150-million year old fish that choked to death on a belemnite rostrum: the hard, internal shell of an extinct, squid-like animal. Original paper. As usual, you can also use...

item.title: NASA did eventually solve Artemis II’s Outlook glitch
item.description: On Thursday, during Artemis II's journey to the Moon, commander Reid Wiseman ran into a tech issue some of us back on Earth can relate to: Microsoft Outlook wasn't working. In a conversation...

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

item.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
item.description: The viral AI agentic tool let attackers silently gain admin unauthenticated access.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
item.description: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

item.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
item.description: OpenAI is undergoing another round of C-suite changes, according to an internal memo viewed by The Verge. Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment - who was until recently the company's CEO of...

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

item.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
item.description: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

item.title: OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
item.description: The company is undergoing major leadership restructuring as its CEO of AGI deployment goes on leave for “several weeks.”

item.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
item.description: A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

item.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 suddenly costs $650 more as RAMageddon lays waste to gaming hardware
item.description: Remember when we thought the Legion Go 2 was expensive at $1,099 and up? Those were the days - Best Buy is now listing Lenovo's handheld for $1,499 with a Ryzen Z2 or $1,999 with a Z2 Extreme. The...

item.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
item.description: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025, following a two-year period of minimal targeting in the region. The campaign has...

item.title: The best iPad deals you can get right now
item.description: While the best iPad deals usually land during major sale events like Black Friday, many great iPad deals are available outside of those moments. The day-to-day discounts come and go like the...

item.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. In January, Elon...

item.title: Mercedes adds steer-by-wire — and a dang steering yoke — to the EQS
item.description: Steer-by-wire, in which a car can be steered electronically rather than through a physical connection between the steering wheel and steering rack, is coming to Mercedes-Benz. The German automaker...

item.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
item.description: Threat actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft...
